sections.label: Lyrics
lines: [Verse 1]
They say the world is safe tonight
Glass towers glowing soft and white
Promises wrapped in neon calm
But underneath, the silence storms
Champagne lies and lullabies
Heavy heads, closed weary eyes
While shadows move without a sound
The truth is burning underground
[Pre-Chorus]
Every whisper says, “Relax, you’re fine”
But peace like that is paper-thin
The clock keeps breathing down our necks
This dark won’t warn us when it ends
[Chorus]
Stay awake, don’t close your eyes
The night breaks in without a sign
They say it’s peace, but it’s a lie
It comes like fire in the quiet sky
Stay awake, don’t fade away
We’re children of the coming day
Not asleep, not numb, not blind
Stay awake—this isn’t the time
[Verse 2]
We’re not made for borrowed dreams
Or drowning in the in-between
You feel it shaking in your chest
A holy unrest, a warning breath
While others drift like fading light
We’re holding onto something bright
There’s more than sleep behind these scars
We were made to see the dark
[Pre-Chorus 2]
If the world goes quiet, hearts seduced
By comfort dressed up as the truth
Then let us be the ones who stand
With burning lamps in open hands
[Chorus]
Stay awake, don’t close your eyes
The night breaks in without a sign
They say it’s peace, but it’s a lie
It comes like fire in the quiet sky
Stay awake, don’t fade away
We’re children of the coming day
Not asleep, not numb, not blind
Stay awake—this isn’t the time
[Bridge]
We don’t belong to the dark
We don’t belong to the haze
Every heartbeat is a spark
Cutting through the end of days
So when the silence starts to scream
And comfort tries to pull us under
We’ll stand awake between the seams
Wide-eyed in the rolling thunder
[Final Chorus / Outro]
Stay awake, the dawn is near
Even if the night feels endless here
Hold the line, don’t trade your sight
For a false calm dressed as light
Stay awake—remember who you are
You were never meant to sleep through stars
